rulings.Case Title: AARON LAM VS. MOSSER COMPANIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against Mosser Parties Due To Excusable Neglect
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT MOSSER COMPANIES, INC., FO 371 TURK STREET PROPERTY LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Default Entered Against Mosser Parties Due To Excusable Neglect is GRANTED pursuant to stipulation. Defendant shall file an answer within 2 days of execution of the order. Defendant to prepare an order and present it for execution at the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES LOUIE VS. CHHAY CHEA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT CHHAY CHEA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The Court will treat this motion as a motion for judgment on the pleadings. The Motion for Judgment on the pleadings is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to comply with CCP 1166(a)(3). Trial is taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YAN YUN CHEN VS. NORMA HERRERA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT NORMA HERRERA, JULIAN RODRIGUEZ DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The operative complaint is not uncertain. Additionally moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that notices of rent increase are "addenda or attachments" within the meaning of CCP 1166. Defendant shall have 5 days from the notice of entry of order to file an answer. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
